attachment.content:
- experimental-research-workflow: Use when designing, implementing, auditing, or reporting experimental research workflows that require rerunnable experiment execution, append-only raw evidence, deterministic transformations, canonical tables, traceable analysis outputs, and paper/report claim evidence. Read references/AGENTS.md first, then load the specific references/docs file needed for the task.
- sleep-dev-sprints: Run PM-led, deadline-bound multi-sprint development on one persistent branch and PR by delegating Codex/Claude roles and governing backlog with GitHub issues, labels, milestones, and PR comments. Use when the user wants Codex to operate like a project manager through repeated 40-60 minute sprints, refill backlog from market/security/clean-code analysis, and continue until a specified local deadline.
- jacob-checkpoint: Run a Jacob-style final checkpoint on completed or nearly completed implementations. Use when Codex has changed code, config, schema, API, behavior, or important implementation-facing docs and must verify preference fit and correctness before finalizing. Trigger on requests such as "final checkpoint", "review as Jacob", "check preference fit and correctness", or when global instructions require a post-implementation gate. Do not use for pure planning, pure exploration, or discussion-only turns.
- afc: Use when they want to quickly load a repository's documentation and source code into the context for implementation-detail questions. Runs scripts/afc.py to emit a budgeted one-shot context dump, defaulting to 160k tokens.
- harness-engineering: Bootstrap or migrate a repository into an agent-first harness-engineering knowledge store modeled on OpenAI's Harness engineering article. Use when Codex needs to set up AGENTS.md plus a structured docs/ system of record for a new repository, or audit an existing repository's documentation against the real codebase, rewrite mismatched docs, retire stale docs, and re-home surviving docs into a predictable agent-readable structure.
- dd: Quick parent directory navigation - go up N levels or to a named ancestor directory
- Notion:find: Quickly find pages or databases in Notion by title keywords.
- Notion:search: Search the user’s Notion workspace using the Notion MCP server and Notion Workspace Skill.
- Notion:database-query: Query a Notion database by name or ID and return structured, readable results.
- Notion:create-page: Create a new Notion page, optionally under a specific parent, using the Notion Workspace Skill and Notion MCP server.
- Notion:create-database-row: Insert a new row into a specified Notion database using natural-language property values.
- Notion:create-task: Create a new task in the user’s Notion tasks database with sensible defaults.
- Notion:tasks:setup: Set up a Notion task board for tracking tasks
- Notion:tasks:explain-diff: Makes a Notion doc explaining a code change.
- Notion:tasks:build: Build a task from a Notion page URL
- Notion:tasks:plan: Plan a task from a Notion page URL
- skill-builder:implement: Build a Claude Code skill from media (video / audio) or text requirements. Transcribes audio with SenseVoice-Small and extracts video keyframes with ffmpeg, composes a requirements brief, then hands off to superpowers:brainstorming. Use when the user wants to create a new skill from a recording, voice memo, walkthrough video, or written notes.
- skill-builder:packager: Package developed skills into client-ready .skill files for non-technical users. Generates installer, healthcheck, and uninstall scripts from a manifest.json declaration. Validates against agentskills.io specification. Use when you need to package, distribute, deliver, or ship a skill to end users who will install via Claude Code desktop's Upload a skill GUI, or when preparing skills for client deployment.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# ThinkAloud
2	
3	Local-first voice input for macOS. Press a hotkey anywhere, dictate, get the text pasted into the focused app — and optionally save the recording + transcript as a personal dataset.
4	
5	Built around [Qwen3-ASR](https://huggingface.co/mlx-community?search_models=Qwen3-ASR) running on-device via [mlx-audio-swift](https://github.com/Blaizzy/mlx-audio-swift). No cloud, no telemetry.
6	
7	## Features
8	
9	- **One hotkey, whole flow.** Default `⌥Space` starts recording → press again to stop & transcribe → press again to insert & save. The three actions are also rebindable separately.
10	- **Streaming transcription** with editable preview before inserting.
11	- **Chinese output preference** — keep model output as-is, prefer Traditional (正體), or prefer Simplified (簡體). Conversion runs locally via ICU.
12	- **Dataset browser** — play recordings, scrub the timeline, edit transcripts, bulk delete.
13	- **Built-in benchmark** — re-run any saved model against your dataset, see CER + exact-match rate + per-character git-style diff. Multiple runs kept for cross-model comparison. JSON export.
14	- **Push to Hugging Face Hub** — full LFS support, repo card auto-generated, token stored in macOS Keychain.
15	- **Idle auto-unload** of model weights to free memory between sessions.
16	
17	## Requirements
18	
19	- macOS 15 (Sequoia) or later
20	- Apple Silicon (M-series)
21	- ~600 MB – 4 GB free disk per model variant
22	- Internet on first run (model download) and for HF push
23	
24	## Quick start
25	
26	1. Launch ThinkAloud — a microphone icon appears in the menu bar.
27	2. Grant **Microphone** and **Accessibility** the first time the system asks (or via Settings → Permissions).
28	3. Click into any text field in any app.
29	4. Press `⌥Space` to start recording.
30	5. Press `⌥Space` to stop and transcribe (streamed live into the popup).
31	6. Press `⌥Space` to insert into the focused app and save to your dataset.
32	
33	(Or just press once and rely on the same key dispatching the right action per phase.)
34	
35	## Settings
36	
37	| Pane | What it controls |
38	| --- | --- |
39	| **Hotkeys** | Per-action keybindings (start / stop / insert + save). All default to `⌥Space`. |
40	| **Permissions** | Microphone + Accessibility status. One-click "Open System Settings". |
41	| **Model** | Quality preset (Fast / Balanced / Accurate Qwen3-ASR variants), Chinese output preference, idle auto-unload timeout, smoke test. |
42	| **Dataset** | Storage stats, open data folder, export JSONL, clear all. Opens the dataset browser. |
43	| **Advanced** | Per-model download/remove. Hugging Face token. |
44	
45	## Dataset browser
46	
47	Open from menu bar **Browse Dataset…** (`⌘D`) or Settings → Dataset.
48	
49	- **Records** — saved dictations. Play, scrub, edit the transcript (raw stays immutable, edited overwrites). Multi-select + `⌘⌫` for bulk delete. Push the whole dataset to HF Hub from the toolbar.
50	- **Benchmark** — pick a model + Chinese preference, run the full pipeline (audio decode → model → post-process) against every record. Reports overall CER, exact-match rate, average latency; per-record diffs show what the model omitted (green underline) vs hallucinated (red strikethrough). Multiple runs are kept in-session for comparison and can be exported as JSON.
51	
52	## Privacy
53	
54	- All audio is processed on-device. Nothing is sent to a server unless you explicitly press **Push** in the dataset browser.
55	- The Hugging Face token is stored in macOS Keychain — never on disk in plain text, never in UserDefaults.
56	- Recordings are only saved to your dataset when you choose **Insert & save**. Plain Insert discards the audio.
57	- The dataset (SQLite + WAV files) lives at `~/Library/Application Support/ThinkAloud/`.
58	
59	## Build from source
60	
61	```bash
62	# Install build tooling
63	brew install xcodegen
64	
65	# Generate the Xcode project from project.yml
66	xcodegen generate
67	
68	# Build
69	xcodebuild -project ThinkAloud.xcodeproj -scheme ThinkAloud -configuration Debug build
70	
71	# Run tests (37 unit tests; one optional live ASR test gated by env)
72	xcodebuild -project ThinkAloud.xcodeproj -scheme ThinkAloud -configuration Debug test
73	```
74	
75	To open in Xcode: `open ThinkAloud.xcodeproj`.
76	
77	The project signs with Developer ID Application (so TCC permissions persist across builds). To build locally, edit `project.yml` and replace `DEVELOPMENT_TEAM` with your own team ID, then regenerate.
78	
79	## Tech stack
80	
81	- SwiftUI + AppKit, Swift 6, macOS 15+
82	- [mlx-audio-swift](https://github.com/Blaizzy/mlx-audio-swift) — Qwen3-ASR runtime
83	- [mlx-swift](https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-swift) — Apple's MLX
84	- [GRDB.swift](https://github.com/groue/GRDB.swift) — SQLite layer
85	- [KeyboardShortcuts](https://github.com/sindresorhus/KeyboardShortcuts) — global hotkeys
86	- [xcodegen](https://github.com/yonaskolb/XcodeGen) — project file generation
87	
88	## Status
89	
90	MVP. Working end-to-end on macOS 15.x / Apple Silicon. Tested with Qwen3-ASR 0.6B-4bit, 1.7B-4bit, and 1.7B-8bit variants.
91

file.content:
# ThinkAloud

Local-first voice input for macOS. Press a hotkey anywhere, dictate, get the text pasted into the focused app — and optionally save the recording + transcript as a personal dataset.

Built around [Qwen3-ASR](https://huggingface.co/mlx-community?search_models=Qwen3-ASR) running on-device via [mlx-audio-swift](https://github.com/Blaizzy/mlx-audio-swift). No cloud, no telemetry.

## Features

- **One hotkey, whole flow.** Default `⌥Space` starts recording → press again to stop & transcribe → press again to insert & save. The three actions are also rebindable separately.
- **Streaming transcription** with editable preview before inserting.
- **Chinese output preference** — keep model output as-is, prefer Traditional (正體), or prefer Simplified (簡體). Conversion runs locally via ICU.
- **Dataset browser** — play recordings, scrub the timeline, edit transcripts, bulk delete.
- **Built-in benchmark** — re-run any saved model against your dataset, see CER + exact-match rate + per-character git-style diff. Multiple runs kept for cross-model comparison. JSON export.
- **Push to Hugging Face Hub** — full LFS support, repo card auto-generated, token stored in macOS Keychain.
- **Idle auto-unload** of model weights to free memory between sessions.

## Requirements

- macOS 15 (Sequoia) or later
- Apple Silicon (M-series)
- ~600 MB – 4 GB free disk per model variant
- Internet on first run (model download) and for HF push

## Quick start

1. Launch ThinkAloud — a microphone icon appears in the menu bar.
2. Grant **Microphone** and **Accessibility** the first time the system asks (or via Settings → Permissions).
3. Click into any text field in any app.
4. Press `⌥Space` to start recording.
5. Press `⌥Space` to stop and transcribe (streamed live into the popup).
6. Press `⌥Space` to insert into the focused app and save to your dataset.

(Or just press once and rely on the same key dispatching the right action per phase.)

## Settings

| Pane | What it controls |
| --- | --- |
| **Hotkeys** | Per-action keybindings (start / stop / insert + save). All default to `⌥Space`. |
| **Permissions** | Microphone + Accessibility status. One-click "Open System Settings". |
| **Model** | Quality preset (Fast / Balanced / Accurate Qwen3-ASR variants), Chinese output preference, idle auto-unload timeout, smoke test. |
| **Dataset** | Storage stats, open data folder, export JSONL, clear all. Opens the dataset browser. |
| **Advanced** | Per-model download/remove. Hugging Face token. |

## Dataset browser

Open from menu bar **Browse Dataset…** (`⌘D`) or Settings → Dataset.

- **Records** — saved dictations. Play, scrub, edit the transcript (raw stays immutable, edited overwrites). Multi-select + `⌘⌫` for bulk delete. Push the whole dataset to HF Hub from the toolbar.
- **Benchmark** — pick a model + Chinese preference, run the full pipeline (audio decode → model → post-process) against every record. Reports overall CER, exact-match rate, average latency; per-record diffs show what the model omitted (green underline) vs hallucinated (red strikethrough). Multiple runs are kept in-session for comparison and can be exported as JSON.

## Privacy

- All audio is processed on-device. Nothing is sent to a server unless you explicitly press **Push** in the dataset browser.
- The Hugging Face token is stored in macOS Keychain — never on disk in plain text, never in UserDefaults.
- Recordings are only saved to your dataset when you choose **Insert & save**. Plain Insert discards the audio.
- The dataset (SQLite + WAV files) lives at `~/Library/Application Support/ThinkAloud/`.

## Build from source

```bash
# Install build tooling
brew install xcodegen

# Generate the Xcode project from project.yml
xcodegen generate

# Build
xcodebuild -project ThinkAloud.xcodeproj -scheme ThinkAloud -configuration Debug build

# Run tests (37 unit tests; one optional live ASR test gated by env)
xcodebuild -project ThinkAloud.xcodeproj -scheme ThinkAloud -configuration Debug test
```

To open in Xcode: `open ThinkAloud.xcodeproj`.

The project signs with Developer ID Application (so TCC permissions persist across builds). To build locally, edit `project.yml` and replace `DEVELOPMENT_TEAM` with your own team ID, then regenerate.

## Tech stack

- SwiftUI + AppKit, Swift 6, macOS 15+
- [mlx-audio-swift](https://github.com/Blaizzy/mlx-audio-swift) — Qwen3-ASR runtime
- [mlx-swift](https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-swift) — Apple's MLX
- [GRDB.swift](https://github.com/groue/GRDB.swift) — SQLite layer
- [KeyboardShortcuts](https://github.com/sindresorhus/KeyboardShortcuts) — global hotkeys
- [xcodegen](https://github.com/yonaskolb/XcodeGen) — project file generation

## Status

MVP. Working end-to-end on macOS 15.x / Apple Silicon. Tested with Qwen3-ASR 0.6B-4bit, 1.7B-4bit, and 1.7B-8bit variants.

name: ThinkAloud
options:
  bundleIdPrefix: com.jacoblincool
  deploymentTarget:
    macOS: "15.0"
  developmentLanguage: en
  createIntermediateGroups: true

settings:
  base:
    SWIFT_VERSION: "6.0"
    MARKETING_VERSION: "0.1.0"

    MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET: "15.0"
    SWIFT_STRICT_CONCURRENCY: complete

packages:

    url: https://github.com/sindresorhus/KeyboardShortcuts
    from: 2.4.0

    branch: add-whisper

    from: 0.30.6

    url: https://github.com/groue/GRDB.swift
    from: 7.10.0

targets:
  ThinkAloud:
    type: application
    platform: macOS
    deploymentTarget: "15.0"
    sources:
      - path: ThinkAloud/App
      - path: ThinkAloud/UI
      - path: ThinkAloud/Core
      - path: ThinkAloud/Models
      - path: ThinkAloud/Resources/Assets.xcassets
        buildPhase: resources
      - path: ThinkAloud/Resources/Localizable.xcstrings
        buildPhase: resources
      - path: ThinkAloud/Resources/InfoPlist.xcstrings
        buildPhase: resources
    info:
      path: ThinkAloud/Resources/Info.plist
      properties:
        CFBundleName: ThinkAloud
        CFBundleDisplayName: ThinkAloud
        CFBundleShortVersionString: $(MARKETING_VERSION)
        CFBundleVersion: $(CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION)
        LSMinimumSystemVersion: $(MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET)
        LSUIElement: true
        NSHumanReadableCopyright: "© 2026 JacobLinCool"
        NSMicrophoneUsageDescription: "ThinkAloud needs microphone access to record your voice for local transcription."
    entitlements:
      path: ThinkAloud/Resources/ThinkAloud.entitlements
      properties:
        com.apple.security.device.audio-input: true
        com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation: true
        com.apple.security.cs.allow-jit: true
        com.apple.security.cs.allow-unsigned-executable-memory: true
        com.apple.security.automation.apple-events: true
    settings:
      base:
        PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER: com.jacoblincool.thinkaloud
        PRODUCT_NAME: ThinkAloud
        CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY: "Developer ID Application"
        CODE_SIGN_STYLE: Manual
        ENABLE_HARDENED_RUNTIME: YES
        DEVELOPMENT_TEAM: "5H75SGA7KP"
        ASSETCATALOG_COMPILER_APPICON_NAME: AppIcon
        COMBINE_HIDPI_IMAGES: YES
        OTHER_CODE_SIGN_FLAGS: "--timestamp=none"
    dependencies:
      - package: KeyboardShortcuts
      - package: mlx-audio-swift
        product: MLXAudioSTT
      - package: mlx-audio-swift
        product: MLXAudioCore
      - package: mlx-swift
        product: MLX
      - package: GRDB
        product: GRDB

  ThinkAloudTests:
    type: bundle.unit-test
    platform: macOS
    deploymentTarget: "15.0"
    sources:
      - path: ThinkAloudTests
    dependencies:
      - target: ThinkAloud
    settings:
      base:
        PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER: com.jacoblincool.thinkaloud.tests
        CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY: "-"
        ENABLE_HARDENED_RUNTIME: NO
        DEVELOPMENT_TEAM: ""
        GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE: YES

schemes:

    build:
      targets:
        ThinkAloud: all
        ThinkAloudTests: [test]
    test:
      targets:
        - ThinkAloudTests
      gatherCoverageData: false
    run:
      config: Debug
    archive:

content.content:
1	# Tag-driven signed + notarized DMG release.
2	#
3	# Triggers on tags matching `v*` (e.g. `v0.2.0`). Also runnable manually for dry runs.
4	#
5	# Required repository secrets:
6	#   DEVELOPER_ID_CERT_P12_BASE64    base64-encoded .p12 export of the
7	#                                   "Developer ID Application: <Name> (5H75SGA7KP)" cert
8	#                                   (Keychain Access → right-click cert → Export, set a password).
9	#                                   `base64 -i cert.p12 | pbcopy` to get the value.
10	#   DEVELOPER_ID_CERT_P12_PASSWORD  the password set when exporting the .p12
11	#   KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD               any random string — used as the temp keychain's password
12	#                                   (rotates per run, never persisted)
13	#   NOTARY_API_KEY_ID               App Store Connect API Key ID (e.g. "ABCDE12345")
14	#   NOTARY_API_ISSUER_ID            ASC API issuer UUID (Users and Access → Integrations)
15	#   NOTARY_API_KEY_P8_BASE64        base64-encoded contents of the AuthKey_*.p8 file
16	#                                   (`base64 -i AuthKey_XXX.p8 | pbcopy`)
17	
18	name: Release
19	
20	on:
21	  push:
22	    tags:
23	      - 'v*'
24	  workflow_dispatch:
25	    inputs:
26	      tag:
27	        description: 'Tag to build (e.g. v0.2.0). For manual dry runs.'
28	        required: true
29	
30	permissions:
31	  contents: write   # gh release create / upload
32	
33	concurrency:
34	  group: release-${{ github.ref }}
35	  cancel-in-progress: false
36	
37	jobs:
38	  build:
39	    runs-on: macos-15
40	    timeout-minutes: 60
41	
42	    steps:
43	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
44	        with:
45	          fetch-depth: 0   # so `gh release --generate-notes` can diff against the previous tag
46	
47	      - name: Resolve version
48	        id: version
49	        run: |
50	          set -euo pipefail
51	          if [[ "${{ github.event_name }}" == "workflow_dispatch" ]]; then
52	            TAG="${{ inputs.tag }}"
53	          else
54	            TAG="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}"
55	          fi
56	          VERSION="${TAG#v}"
57	          echo "tag=$TAG"                              >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
58	          echo "version=$VERSION"                     >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
59	          echo "build=${GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER}"           >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
60	          echo "Building $TAG (MARKETING_VERSION=$VERSION, CFBundleVersion=$GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER)"
61	
62	      - name: Select latest Xcode
63	        run: |
64	          set -euo pipefail
65	          # mlx-audio-swift's Package.swift uses `swift-tools-version:6.2`, which requires
66	          # Xcode 16.4+. The macos-15 image's default xcode-select may be on 16.1 (Swift 6.1).
67	          LATEST=$(ls -d /Applications/Xcode_*.app | sort -V | tail -1)
68	          echo "Selecting $LATEST"
69	          sudo xcode-select -s "$LATEST"
70	          xcodebuild -version
71	          swift --version
72	
73	      - name: Install build deps
74	        run: brew install xcodegen
75	
76	      - name: Generate Xcode project

file.content:
# Tag-driven signed + notarized DMG release.
#
# Triggers on tags matching `v*` (e.g. `v0.2.0`). Also runnable manually for dry runs.
#
# Required repository secrets:
#   DEVELOPER_ID_CERT_P12_BASE64    base64-encoded .p12 export of the
#                                   "Developer ID Application: <Name> (5H75SGA7KP)" cert
#                                   (Keychain Access → right-click cert → Export, set a password).
#                                   `base64 -i cert.p12 | pbcopy` to get the value.
#   DEVELOPER_ID_CERT_P12_PASSWORD  the password set when exporting the .p12
#   KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD               any random string — used as the temp keychain's password
#                                   (rotates per run, never persisted)
#   NOTARY_API_KEY_ID               App Store Connect API Key ID (e.g. "ABCDE12345")
#   NOTARY_API_ISSUER_ID            ASC API issuer UUID (Users and Access → Integrations)
#   NOTARY_API_KEY_P8_BASE64        base64-encoded contents of the AuthKey_*.p8 file
#                                   (`base64 -i AuthKey_XXX.p8 | pbcopy`)

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: 'Tag to build (e.g. v0.2.0). For manual dry runs.'
        required: true

permissions:
  contents: write   # gh release create / upload

concurrency:
  group: release-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 60

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0   # so `gh release --generate-notes` can diff against the previous tag

      - name: Resolve version
        id: version
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [[ "${{ github.event_name }}" == "workflow_dispatch" ]]; then
            TAG="${{ inputs.tag }}"
          else
            TAG="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}"
          fi
          VERSION="${TAG#v}"
          echo "tag=$TAG"                              >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=$VERSION"                     >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "build=${GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER}"           >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Building $TAG (MARKETING_VERSION=$VERSION, CFBundleVersion=$GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER)"

      - name: Select latest Xcode
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # mlx-audio-swift's Package.swift uses `swift-tools-version:6.2`, which requires
          # Xcode 16.4+. The macos-15 image's default xcode-select may be on 16.1 (Swift 6.1).
          LATEST=$(ls -d /Applications/Xcode_*.app | sort -V | tail -1)
          echo "Selecting $LATEST"
          sudo xcode-select -s "$LATEST"
          xcodebuild -version
          swift --version

      - name: Install build deps
        run: brew install xcodegen

      - name: Generate Xcode project
        run: xcodegen generate

      - name: Import signing certificate
        env:
          P12_BASE64:    ${{ secrets.DEVELOPER_ID_CERT_P12_BASE64 }}
          P12_PASSWORD:  ${{ secrets.DEVELOPER_ID_CERT_P12_PASSWORD }}
          KEYCHAIN_PASS: ${{ secrets.KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          KEYCHAIN="$RUNNER_TEMP/build.keychain-db"

          security create-keychain -p "$KEYCHAIN_PASS" "$KEYCHAIN"
          security set-keychain-settings -lut 21600 "$KEYCHAIN"
          security unlock-keychain  -p "$KEYCHAIN_PASS" "$KEYCHAIN"

          echo "$P12_BASE64" | base64 --decode > "$RUNNER_TEMP/cert.p12"
          security import "$RUNNER_TEMP/cert.p12" \
            -P "$P12_PASSWORD" \
            -A -t cert -f pkcs12 \
            -k "$KEYCHAIN"
          rm -f "$RUNNER_TEMP/cert.p12"

          # Prepend our keychain to the search list so codesign finds it.
          security list-keychains -d user -s "$KEYCHAIN" $(security list-keychains -d user | tr -d '"')

          # Allow codesign / productsign to use the key without a UI prompt.
          security set-key-partition-list -S apple-tool:,apple: -k "$KEYCHAIN_PASS" "$KEYCHAIN" >/dev/null

          # Surface the identity that ended up installed so a missing cert is obvious in the log.
          security find-identity -v -p codesigning "$KEYCHAIN"

          echo "BUILD_KEYCHAIN=$KEYCHAIN" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: Archive
        env:
          MARKETING_VERSION:       ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
          CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.build }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          xcodebuild archive \
            -project ThinkAloud.xcodeproj \
            -scheme ThinkAloud \
            -configuration Release \
            -archivePath "$RUNNER_TEMP/ThinkAloud.xcarchive" \
            -destination 'generic/platform=macOS' \
            MARKETING_VERSION="$MARKETING_VERSION" \
            CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION="$CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION" \
            CODE_SIGN_STYLE=Manual \
            CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="Developer ID Application" \
            DEVELOPMENT_TEAM=5H75SGA7KP \
            OTHER_CODE_SIGN_FLAGS="--timestamp" \
            COMPILER_INDEX_STORE_ENABLE=NO

      - name: Export .app
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          xcodebuild -exportArchive \
            -archivePath "$RUNNER_TEMP/ThinkAloud.xcarchive" \
            -exportOptionsPlist scripts/ExportOptions.plist \
            -exportPath "$RUNNER_TEMP/export"
          ls -la "$RUNNER_TEMP/export"

      - name: Verify .app signature + hardened runtime
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          APP="$RUNNER_TEMP/export/ThinkAloud.app"
          codesign --verify --strict --deep --verbose=2 "$APP"
          codesign --display --verbose=4 "$APP" 2>&1 | grep -E 'Authority|Timestamp|Runtime' || true
          # Confirms `flags=0x10000(runtime)` is present — required by the notary.
          codesign --display --verbose=2 "$APP" 2>&1 | grep -q 'runtime' \
            || { echo "::error::Hardened runtime missing from signed .app"; exit 1; }

      - name: Build DMG
        id: dmg
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
          APP="$RUNNER_TEMP/export/ThinkAloud.app"
          DMG_NAME="ThinkAloud-$VERSION.dmg"
          DMG_PATH="$RUNNER_TEMP/$DMG_NAME"
          STAGING="$RUNNER_TEMP/dmg-staging"

          mkdir -p "$STAGING"
          cp -R "$APP" "$STAGING/"
          ln -s /Applications "$STAGING/Applications"   # drag-to-install affordance

          hdiutil create \
            -volname "ThinkAloud" \
            -srcfolder "$STAGING" \
            -ov -format UDZO \
            "$DMG_PATH"

          echo "path=$DMG_PATH" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "name=$DMG_NAME" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Sign DMG
        run: |
          codesign --sign "Developer ID Application" --timestamp \
            "${{ steps.dmg.outputs.path }}"
          codesign --verify --verbose=2 "${{ steps.dmg.outputs.path }}"

      - name: Notarize DMG
        id: notarize
        env:
          ASC_API_KEY_ID:     ${{ secrets.NOTARY_API_KEY_ID }}
          ASC_API_ISSUER_ID:  ${{ secrets.NOTARY_API_ISSUER_ID }}
          ASC_API_KEY_BASE64: ${{ secrets.NOTARY_API_KEY_P8_BASE64 }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          KEY_PATH="$RUNNER_TEMP/AuthKey.p8"
          echo "$ASC_API_KEY_BASE64" | base64 --decode > "$KEY_PATH"

          # `notarytool submit --wait` returns non-zero only on transport errors. A submission
          # accepted-by-server-but-rejected-by-notary returns 0 with status=Invalid, so we parse
          # the JSON output and decide ourselves whether to fail.
          SUBMIT_JSON=$(xcrun notarytool submit "${{ steps.dmg.outputs.path }}" \
            --key "$KEY_PATH" \
            --key-id "$ASC_API_KEY_ID" \
            --issuer "$ASC_API_ISSUER_ID" \
            --wait \
            --output-format json)
          echo "$SUBMIT_JSON"

          SUBMISSION_ID=$(printf '%s' "$SUBMIT_JSON" | jq -r '.id')
          STATUS=$(printf '%s' "$SUBMIT_JSON" | jq -r '.status')
          echo "submission_id=$SUBMISSION_ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          if [[ "$STATUS" != "Accepted" ]]; then
            echo "::error::Notarization status: $STATUS — dumping log."
            xcrun notarytool log "$SUBMISSION_ID" \
              --key "$KEY_PATH" --key-id "$ASC_API_KEY_ID" --issuer "$ASC_API_ISSUER_ID"
            rm -f "$KEY_PATH"
            exit 1
          fi
          rm -f "$KEY_PATH"

      - name: Staple + Gatekeeper assess
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          xcrun stapler staple "${{ steps.dmg.outputs.path }}"
          xcrun stapler validate "${{ steps.dmg.outputs.path }}"
          spctl --assess --type open --context context:primary-signature -v \
            "${{ steps.dmg.outputs.path }}"

      - name: Publish GitHub Release
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          TAG="${{ steps.version.outputs.tag }}"
          DMG="${{ steps.dmg.outputs.path }}"
          if gh release view "$TAG" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Release $TAG already exists — uploading asset with --clobber."
            gh release upload "$TAG" "$DMG" --clobber
          else
            gh release create "$TAG" "$DMG" \
              --title "ThinkAloud ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}" \
              --generate-notes
          fi

      - name: Upload DMG as build artifact
        if: steps.dmg.outputs.path != ''
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ${{ steps.dmg.outputs.name }}
          path: ${{ steps.dmg.outputs.path }}
          retention-days: 14

      - name: Clean up keychain
        if: always()
        run: security delete-keychain "$BUILD_KEYCHAIN" || true

content.subject: Audit Swift app code (ThinkAloud) for security issues
content.description: Review networking, IPC, file I/O, permissions, microphone usage, model loading, sandbox/entitlements, URL handling, unsafe APIs, and accessibility.

content.subject: Audit CI workflows and release pipeline
content.description: Review .github/workflows for permissions, third-party action pinning, secret exposure, artifact handling, signing/notarization flow.

content.subject: Audit scripts and build configuration
content.description: Review scripts/, project.yml, build/, entitlements, signing identity, and notarization scripts for unsafe patterns.

content.subject: Compile findings into prioritized report

1	import CryptoKit
2	import Foundation
3	
4	/// Minimal Hugging Face Hub API client. Implements just the endpoints needed to push a
5	/// ThinkAloud dataset (whoami / create repo / preupload / LFS batch / S3 PUT / commit).
6	/// All requests authenticate with a personal access token.
7	actor HFHubClient {
8	    enum HFError: Error, LocalizedError {
9	        case httpStatus(Int, String)
10	        case malformedResponse(String)
11	        case lfsError(String)
12	
13	        var errorDescription: String? {
14	            switch self {
15	            case .httpStatus(let code, let body):
16	                let snippet = body.count > 240 ? String(body.prefix(240)) + "…" : body
17	                return "HTTP \(code) — \(snippet)"
18	            case .malformedResponse(let msg):
19	                return "Malformed response: \(msg)"
20	            case .lfsError(let msg):
21	                return "LFS: \(msg)"
22	            }
23	        }
24	    }
25	
26	    private let token: String
27	    private let session: URLSession
28	    private let base = URL(string: "https://huggingface.co")!
29	
30	    init(token: String, session: URLSession = .shared) {
31	        self.token = token
32	        self.session = session
33	    }
34	
35	    // MARK: - whoami
36	
37	    struct WhoAmI: Decodable, Sendable {
38	        let name: String
39	        let email: String?
40	        let type: String?
41	    }
42	
43	    func whoami() async throws -> WhoAmI {
44	        let url = base.appendingPathComponent("api/whoami-v2")
45	        let (data, response) = try await get(url)
46	        try assertOK(response, data: data)
47	        return try JSONDecoder().decode(WhoAmI.self, from: data)
48	    }
49	
50	    // MARK: - Create repo
51	
52	    func createDatasetRepo(name: String, organization: String?, isPrivate: Bool) async throws {
53	        let url = base.appendingPathComponent("api/repos/create")
54	        var body: [String: Any] = [
55	            "type": "dataset",
56	            "name": name,
57	            "private": isPrivate
58	        ]
59	        if let organization { body["organization"] = organization }
60	        let (data, response) = try await post(url, json: body)
61	        if let http = response as? HTTPURLResponse, http.statusCode == 409 {
62	            // Already exists — fine, push will overwrite contents.
63	            return
64	        }
65	        try assertOK(response, data: data)
66	    }
67	
68	    // MARK: - Preupload (dedup + LFS routing)
69	
70	    struct PreuploadFileEntry: Sendable {
71	        let path: String
72	        let sampleBase64: String
73	        let size: Int
74	    }
75	
76	    struct PreuploadResponseFile: Sendable {
77	        let path: String
78	        let uploadMode: String     // "regular" or "lfs"
79	        let shouldIgnore: Bool
80	    }
81	
82	    func preupload(repoID: String, files: [PreuploadFileEntry], revision: String = "main") async throws -> [PreuploadResponseFile] {
83	        let url = base.appendingPathComponent("api/datasets/\(repoID)/preupload/\(revision)")
84	        let body: [String: Any] = [
85	            "files": files.map { [
86	                "path": $0.path,
87	                "sample": $0.sampleBase64,
88	                "size": $0.size
89	            ] }
90	        ]
91	        let (data, response) = try await post(url, json: body)
92	        try assertOK(response, data: data)
93	        guard let obj = try JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: data) as? [String: Any],
94	              let arr = obj["files"] as? [[String: Any]] else {
95	            throw HFError.malformedResponse("preupload missing files[]")
96	        }
97	        return arr.map {
98	            PreuploadResponseFile(
99	                path: ($0["path"] as? String) ?? "",
100	                uploadMode: ($0["uploadMode"] as? String) ?? "regular",
101	                shouldIgnore: ($0["shouldIgnore"] as? Bool) ?? false
102	            )
103	        }
104	    }
105	
106	    // MARK: - LFS batch + S3 PUT + verify
107	
108	    struct LFSObject: Sendable {
109	        let oid: String      // sha256 hex
110	        let size: Int
111	    }
112	
113	    struct LFSUploadInstruction: Sendable {
114	        let oid: String
115	        let size: Int
116	        let uploadURL: URL
117	        let uploadHeaders: [String: String]
118	        let verifyURL: URL?
119	        let verifyHeaders: [String: String]
120	    }
121	
122	    /// Asks HF where to PUT each LFS object. Objects already on the server come back with no
123	    /// "upload" action — caller can skip them.
124	    func lfsBatch(repoID: String, objects: [LFSObject], revision: String = "main") async throws -> [LFSUploadInstruction] {
125	        // Git LFS protocol path: <type>/<repo>.git/info/lfs/objects/batch
126	        let url = base.appendingPathComponent("datasets/\(repoID).git/info/lfs/objects/batch")
127	        let body: [String: Any] = [
128	            "operation": "upload",
129	            "transfers": ["basic"],
130	            "ref": ["name": "refs/heads/\(revision)"],
131	            "objects": objects.map { ["oid": $0.oid, "size": $0.size] }
132	        ]
133	        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
134	        request.httpMethod = "POST"
135	        request.setValue("Bearer \(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
136	        request.setValue("application/vnd.git-lfs+json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Content-Type")
137	        request.setValue("application/vnd.git-lfs+json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Accept")
138	        request.httpBody = try JSONSerialization.data(withJSONObject: body)
139	        let (data, response) = try await session.data(for: request)
140	        try assertOK(response, data: data)
141	
142	        guard let obj = try JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: data) as? [String: Any],
143	              let arr = obj["objects"] as? [[String: Any]] else {
144	            throw HFError.lfsError("batch response missing objects[]")
145	        }
146	        var instructions: [LFSUploadInstruction] = []
147	        for entry in arr {
148	            let oid = (entry["oid"] as? String) ?? ""
149	            let size = (entry["size"] as? Int) ?? 0
150	            if let err = entry["error"] as? [String: Any] {
151	                let msg = (err["message"] as? String) ?? "unknown"
152	                throw HFError.lfsError("oid=\(oid): \(msg)")
153	            }
154	            guard let actions = entry["actions"] as? [String: Any] else {
155	                // No actions → object already exists on server, skip.
156	                continue
157	            }
158	            guard let upload = actions["upload"] as? [String: Any],
159	                  let hrefStr = upload["href"] as? String,
160	                  let uploadURL = URL(string: hrefStr) else {
161	                throw HFError.lfsError("missing upload.href")
162	            }
163	            let uploadHeaders = (upload["header"] as? [String: String]) ?? [:]
164	
165	            var verifyURL: URL?
166	            var verifyHeaders: [String: String] = [:]
167	            if let verify = actions["verify"] as? [String: Any],
168	               let vhref = verify["href"] as? String,
169	               let vurl = URL(string: vhref) {
170	                verifyURL = vurl
171	                verifyHeaders = (verify["header"] as? [String: String]) ?? [:]
172	            }
173	
174	            instructions.append(LFSUploadInstruction(
175	                oid: oid,
176	                size: size,
177	                uploadURL: uploadURL,
178	                uploadHeaders: uploadHeaders,
179	                verifyURL: verifyURL,
180	                verifyHeaders: verifyHeaders
181	            ))
182	        }
183	        return instructions
184	    }
185	
186	    /// PUTs raw bytes to the LFS upload URL (typically a presigned S3 URL).
187	    func lfsUpload(instruction: LFSUploadInstruction, fileURL: URL) async throws {
188	        var request = URLRequest(url: instruction.uploadURL)
189	        request.httpMethod = "PUT"
190	        for (k, v) in instruction.uploadHeaders {
191	            request.setValue(v, forHTTPHeaderField: k)
192	        }
193	        // Use uploadTask(from: file) so large files stream rather than load into RAM.
194	        let (data, response) = try await session.upload(for: request, fromFile: fileURL)
195	        try assertOK(response, data: data)
196	    }
197	
198	    /// Optional but recommended: tells HF the LFS object has been uploaded so it can register it.
199	    func lfsVerify(instruction: LFSUploadInstruction) async throws {
200	        guard let verifyURL = instruction.verifyURL else { return }
201	        var request = URLRequest(url: verifyURL)
202	        request.httpMethod = "POST"
203	        request.setValue("Bearer \(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
204	        request.setValue("application/vnd.git-lfs+json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Content-Type")
205	        for (k, v) in instruction.verifyHeaders {
206	            request.setValue(v, forHTTPHeaderField: k)
207	        }
208	        let body: [String: Any] = ["oid": instruction.oid, "size": instruction.size]
209	        request.httpBody = try JSONSerialization.data(withJSONObject: body)
210	        let (data, response) = try await session.data(for: request)
211	        try assertOK(response, data: data)
212	    }
213	
214	    // MARK: - Commit (NDJSON)
215	
216	    enum CommitOp: Sendable {
217	        case header(summary: String, description: String?)
218	        case fileBase64(path: String, content: Data)
219	        case lfsFile(path: String, oid: String, size: Int)
220	        case deletedFile(path: String)
221	    }
222	
223	    struct CommitResult: Sendable {
224	        let commitOid: String?
225	        let commitURL: String?
226	        let pullRequestURL: String?
227	    }
228	
229	    func commit(repoID: String, operations: [CommitOp], revision: String = "main") async throws -> CommitResult {
230	        let url = base.appendingPathComponent("api/datasets/\(repoID)/commit/\(revision)")
231	        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
232	        request.httpMethod = "POST"
233	        request.setValue("Bearer \(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
234	        request.setValue("application/x-ndjson", forHTTPHeaderField: "Content-Type")
235	
236	        var lines: [String] = []
237	        for op in operations {
238	            let dict: [String: Any]
239	            switch op {
240	            case .header(let summary, let description):
241	                var v: [String: Any] = ["summary": summary]
242	                if let description { v["description"] = description }
243	                dict = ["key": "header", "value": v]
244	            case .fileBase64(let path, let content):
245	                dict = [
246	                    "key": "file",
247	                    "value": [
248	                        "path": path,
249	                        "content": content.base64EncodedString(),
250	                        "encoding": "base64"
251	                    ] as [String: Any]
252	                ]
253	            case .lfsFile(let path, let oid, let size):
254	                dict = [
255	                    "key": "lfsFile",
256	                    "value": [
257	                        "path": path,
258	                        "oid": oid,
259	                        "size": size,
260	                        "algo": "sha256"
261	                    ] as [String: Any]
262	                ]
263	            case .deletedFile(let path):
264	                dict = ["key": "deletedFile", "value": ["path": path]]
265	            }
266	            let data = try JSONSerialization.data(withJSONObject: dict)
267	            guard let line = String(data: data, encoding: .utf8) else {
268	                throw HFError.malformedResponse("commit op encoding failed")
269	            }
270	            lines.append(line)
271	        }
272	        request.httpBody = Data(lines.joined(separator: "\n").utf8)
273	
274	        let (data, response) = try await session.data(for: request)
275	        try assertOK(response, data: data)
276	        let obj = try? JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: data) as? [String: Any]
277	        return CommitResult(
278	            commitOid: obj?["commitOid"] as? String,
279	            commitURL: obj?["commitUrl"] as? String,
280	            pullRequestURL: obj?["pullRequestUrl"] as? String
281	        )
282	    }
283	
284	    // MARK: - SHA256 streaming
285	
286	    /// SHA256 hex + size over a file's contents, computed in 1 MB chunks so large WAVs don't load into RAM.
287	    nonisolated static func sha256Hex(fileURL: URL) throws -> (oid: String, size: Int) {
288	        let handle = try FileHandle(forReadingFrom: fileURL)
289	        defer { try? handle.close() }
290	        var hasher = SHA256()
291	        var total = 0
292	        while autoreleasepool(invoking: { () -> Bool in
293	            do {
294	                guard let chunk = try handle.read(upToCount: 1024 * 1024), !chunk.isEmpty else { return false }
295	                hasher.update(data: chunk)
296	                total += chunk.count
297	                return true
298	            } catch {
299	                return false
300	            }
301	        }) {}
302	        let digest = hasher.finalize()
303	        let hex = digest.map { String(format: "%02x", $0) }.joined()
304	        return (hex, total)
305	    }
306	
307	    /// First-N-bytes sample as base64 (HF uses this for preupload dedup hint).
308	    nonisolated static func sampleBase64(fileURL: URL, bytes: Int = 512) throws -> String {
309	        let handle = try FileHandle(forReadingFrom: fileURL)
310	        defer { try? handle.close() }
311	        let data = try handle.read(upToCount: bytes) ?? Data()
312	        return data.base64EncodedString()
313	    }
314	
315	    // MARK: - Helpers
316	
317	    private func get(_ url: URL) async throws -> (Data, URLResponse) {
318	        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
319	        request.httpMethod = "GET"
320	        request.setValue("Bearer \(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
321	        request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Accept")
322	        return try await session.data(for: request)
323	    }
324	
325	    private func post(_ url: URL, json body: [String: Any]) async throws -> (Data, URLResponse) {
326	        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
327	        request.httpMethod = "POST"
328	        request.setValue("Bearer \(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
329	        request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Content-Type")
330	        request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Accept")
331	        request.httpBody = try JSONSerialization.data(withJSONObject: body)
332	        return try await session.data(for: request)
333	    }
334	
335	    private func assertOK(_ response: URLResponse, data: Data) throws {
336	        guard let http = response as? HTTPURLResponse else {
337	            throw HFError.malformedResponse("not an HTTP response")
338	        }
339	        if !(200..<300).contains(http.statusCode) {
340	            let body = String(data: data, encoding: .utf8) ?? ""
341	            throw HFError.httpStatus(http.statusCode, body)
342	        }
343	    }
344	}
345

1	import Foundation
2	import Security
3	
4	/// Minimal Keychain wrapper for the Hugging Face token. We never persist the token in
5	/// UserDefaults — Keychain only, accessible-after-first-unlock so it survives reboots but
6	/// stays encrypted at rest.
7	enum HFKeychain {
8	    static let defaultService = "com.jacoblincool.thinkaloud.huggingface"
9	    static let defaultAccount = "token"
10	
11	    enum KeychainError: Error, LocalizedError {
12	        case unhandled(OSStatus)
13	
14	        var errorDescription: String? {
15	            switch self {
16	            case .unhandled(let status):
17	                return "Keychain error \(status)"
18	            }
19	        }
20	    }
21	
22	    static func set(_ value: String, service: String = defaultService, account: String = defaultAccount) throws {
23	        let data = Data(value.utf8)
24	        let query: [String: Any] = [
25	            kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
26	            kSecAttrService as String: service,
27	            kSecAttrAccount as String: account
28	        ]
29	        // Overwrite by deleting + re-adding. SecItemUpdate would also work but this is simpler.
30	        SecItemDelete(query as CFDictionary)
31	        var attrs = query
32	        attrs[kSecValueData as String] = data
33	        attrs[kSecAttrAccessible as String] = kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlock
34	        let status = SecItemAdd(attrs as CFDictionary, nil)
35	        if status != errSecSuccess {
36	            throw KeychainError.unhandled(status)
37	        }
38	    }
39	
40	    static func get(service: String = defaultService, account: String = defaultAccount) -> String? {
41	        let query: [String: Any] = [
42	            kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
43	            kSecAttrService as String: service,
44	            kSecAttrAccount as String: account,
45	            kSecReturnData as String: true,
46	            kSecMatchLimit as String: kSecMatchLimitOne
47	        ]
48	        var result: AnyObject?
49	        let status = SecItemCopyMatching(query as CFDictionary, &result)
50	        guard status == errSecSuccess, let data = result as? Data else { return nil }
51	        return String(data: data, encoding: .utf8)
52	    }
53	
54	    static func delete(service: String = defaultService, account: String = defaultAccount) throws {
55	        let query: [String: Any] = [
56	            kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
57	            kSecAttrService as String: service,
58	            kSecAttrAccount as String: account
59	        ]
60	        let status = SecItemDelete(query as CFDictionary)
61	        if status != errSecSuccess && status != errSecItemNotFound {
62	            throw KeychainError.unhandled(status)
63	        }
64	    }
65	}

1	import Foundation
2	
3	struct HFPushOptions: Sendable {
4	    let repoName: String                 // e.g. "thinkaloud-personal"
5	    let organization: String?            // nil → personal namespace
6	    let isPrivate: Bool
7	    let includeAudio: Bool
8	
9	    /// Full repo id as the API expects it ("<owner>/<name>").
10	    func repoID(defaultOwner: String) -> String {
11	        let owner = organization ?? defaultOwner
12	        return "\(owner)/\(repoName)"
13	    }
14	}
15	
16	/// One staged file ready to be sent in a commit. We resolve regular vs LFS routing per file
17	/// based on preupload's response, then either base64-inline (regular) or LFS-upload + lfsFile
18	/// reference (LFS).
19	struct HFPushProgress: Sendable {
20	    enum Stage: String, Sendable {
21	        case prepare
22	        case createRepo
23	        case preupload
24	        case lfsUpload
25	        case commit
26	        case done
27	    }
28	    let stage: Stage
29	    let completed: Int
30	    let total: Int
31	    let currentLabel: String
32	}
33	
34	struct HFPushResult: Sendable {
35	    let repoID: String
36	    let commitURL: String?
37	    let pageURL: String   // human-friendly dataset page
38	    let uploadedFileCount: Int
39	}
40	
41	actor HFPushService {
42	    private let client: HFHubClient
43	    private let datasetStore: DatasetStore
44	    private let audioFileStore: AudioFileStore
45	    private let defaultOwner: String   // from whoami
46	
47	    init(client: HFHubClient, datasetStore: DatasetStore, audioFileStore: AudioFileStore, defaultOwner: String) {
48	        self.client = client
49	        self.datasetStore = datasetStore
50	        self.audioFileStore = audioFileStore
51	        self.defaultOwner = defaultOwner
52	    }
53	
54	    func push(options: HFPushOptions, progress: @Sendable (HFPushProgress) async -> Void = { _ in }) async throws -> HFPushResult {
55	        let repoID = options.repoID(defaultOwner: defaultOwner)
56	
57	        // 1. Pull records + stage files on disk.
58	        await progress(HFPushProgress(stage: .prepare, completed: 0, total: 0, currentLabel: ""))
59	        let records = try await datasetStore.all()
60	
61	        let staging = try makeStagingDirectory()
62	        defer { try? FileManager.default.removeItem(at: staging) }
63	
64	        let metadataURL = staging.appendingPathComponent("metadata.jsonl")
65	        let readmeURL = staging.appendingPathComponent("README.md")
66	        try writeMetadataJSONL(records: records, to: metadataURL)
67	        try writeReadme(records: records, to: readmeURL)
68	
69	        var stagedFiles: [(repoPath: String, localURL: URL)] = [
70	            ("metadata.jsonl", metadataURL),
71	            ("README.md", readmeURL)
72	        ]
73	        if options.includeAudio {
74	            for record in records {
75	                let src = await audioFileStore.absoluteURL(for: record.audioPath)
76	                guard FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: src.path) else { continue }
77	                // Upload at the exact relative path the DB stores in audio_path, so
78	                // metadata.jsonl (which encodes record.audioPath as-is) resolves correctly
79	                // against the repo layout. AudioFileStore guarantees the path starts with
80	                // "audio/" so it sits cleanly inside the dataset repo.
81	                stagedFiles.append((record.audioPath, src))
82	            }
83	        }
84	
85	        // 2. Create repo (idempotent).
86	        await progress(HFPushProgress(stage: .createRepo, completed: 0, total: 1, currentLabel: repoID))
87	        try await client.createDatasetRepo(name: options.repoName, organization: options.organization, isPrivate: options.isPrivate)
88	
89	        // 3. Preupload to determine regular vs LFS routing.
90	        await progress(HFPushProgress(stage: .preupload, completed: 0, total: stagedFiles.count, currentLabel: ""))
91	        let preuploadEntries: [HFHubClient.PreuploadFileEntry] = try stagedFiles.map { staged in
92	            let attrs = try FileManager.default.attributesOfItem(atPath: staged.localURL.path)
93	            let size = (attrs[.size] as? Int) ?? 0
94	            let sample = try HFHubClient.sampleBase64(fileURL: staged.localURL, bytes: 512)
95	            return .init(path: staged.repoPath, sampleBase64: sample, size: size)
96	        }
97	        let routing = try await client.preupload(repoID: repoID, files: preuploadEntries)
98	        let routingByPath = Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues: routing.map { ($0.path, $0) })
99	
100	        // 4. LFS upload pass for files routed to LFS.
101	        var commitOps: [HFHubClient.CommitOp] = [
102	            .header(summary: "Push from ThinkAloud", description: "Uploaded \(records.count) records on \(ISO8601DateFormatter().string(from: Date())).")
103	        ]
104	
105	        let lfsFiles = stagedFiles.filter { (routingByPath[$0.repoPath]?.uploadMode ?? "regular") == "lfs" }
106	        var lfsObjects: [(repoPath: String, localURL: URL, oid: String, size: Int)] = []
107	        for (i, staged) in lfsFiles.enumerated() {
108	            await progress(HFPushProgress(stage: .lfsUpload, completed: i, total: lfsFiles.count, currentLabel: staged.repoPath))
109	            try Task.checkCancellation()
110	            let (oid, size) = try HFHubClient.sha256Hex(fileURL: staged.localURL)
111	            lfsObjects.append((staged.repoPath, staged.localURL, oid, size))
112	        }
113	
114	        if !lfsObjects.isEmpty {
115	            let batch = try await client.lfsBatch(
116	                repoID: repoID,
117	                objects: lfsObjects.map { .init(oid: $0.oid, size: $0.size) }
118	            )
119	            let instructionsByOid = Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues: batch.map { ($0.oid, $0) })
120	            for (i, obj) in lfsObjects.enumerated() {
121	                try Task.checkCancellation()
122	                await progress(HFPushProgress(stage: .lfsUpload, completed: i, total: lfsObjects.count, currentLabel: obj.repoPath))
123	                if let inst = instructionsByOid[obj.oid] {
124	                    try await client.lfsUpload(instruction: inst, fileURL: obj.localURL)
125	                    try await client.lfsVerify(instruction: inst)
126	                }
127	                // If no instruction returned, object already exists on the server — skip upload.
128	                commitOps.append(.lfsFile(path: obj.repoPath, oid: obj.oid, size: obj.size))
129	            }
130	        }
131	
132	        // 5. Inline-base64 the small files into the commit.
133	        let regularFiles = stagedFiles.filter { (routingByPath[$0.repoPath]?.uploadMode ?? "regular") != "lfs" }
134	        for staged in regularFiles {
135	            let data = try Data(contentsOf: staged.localURL)
136	            commitOps.append(.fileBase64(path: staged.repoPath, content: data))
137	        }
138	
139	        // 6. Commit.
140	        await progress(HFPushProgress(stage: .commit, completed: 0, total: 1, currentLabel: repoID))
141	        let result = try await client.commit(repoID: repoID, operations: commitOps)
142	
143	        let pageURL = "https://huggingface.co/datasets/\(repoID)"
144	        await progress(HFPushProgress(stage: .done, completed: stagedFiles.count, total: stagedFiles.count, currentLabel: repoID))
145	        return HFPushResult(repoID: repoID, commitURL: result.commitURL, pageURL: pageURL, uploadedFileCount: stagedFiles.count)
146	    }
147	
148	    // MARK: - Staging file generation
149	
150	    private func makeStagingDirectory() throws -> URL {
151	        let dir = FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory.appendingPathComponent("ThinkAloudPush-\(UUID().uuidString)", isDirectory: true)
152	        try FileManager.default.createDirectory(at: dir, withIntermediateDirectories: true)
153	        return dir
154	    }
155	
156	    private func writeMetadataJSONL(records: [DatasetRecord], to url: URL) throws {
157	        let encoder = JSONEncoder()
158	        encoder.outputFormatting = [.withoutEscapingSlashes]
159	        var lines: [String] = []
160	        for record in records {
161	            let data = try encoder.encode(record)
162	            guard let line = String(data: data, encoding: .utf8) else { continue }
163	            lines.append(line)
164	        }
165	        let text = lines.joined(separator: "\n") + "\n"
166	        try Data(text.utf8).write(to: url, options: .atomic)
167	    }
168	
169	    private func writeReadme(records: [DatasetRecord], to url: URL) throws {
170	        let langCounts = Dictionary(grouping: records, by: { $0.language ?? "unknown" }).mapValues(\.count)
171	        let modelCounts = Dictionary(grouping: records, by: { $0.asrModel }).mapValues(\.count)
172	        var text = """
173	        ---
174	        license: other
175	        task_categories:
176	        - automatic-speech-recognition
177	        ---
178	
179	        # ThinkAloud personal dataset
180	
181	        Generated by [ThinkAloud](https://github.com/JacobLinCool/think-aloud) on \(ISO8601DateFormatter().string(from: Date())).
182	
183	        ## Stats
184	
185	        - Total records: \(records.count)
186	
187	        """
188	        if !langCounts.isEmpty {
189	            text += "\n### Languages\n\n"
190	            for (lang, n) in langCounts.sorted(by: { $0.value > $1.value }) {
191	                text += "- \(lang): \(n)\n"
192	            }
193	        }
194	        if !modelCounts.isEmpty {
195	            text += "\n### Models\n\n"
196	            for (model, n) in modelCounts.sorted(by: { $0.value > $1.value }) {
197	                text += "- \(model): \(n)\n"
198	            }
199	        }
200	        text += """
201	
202	        ## Layout
203	
204	        - `metadata.jsonl` — one JSON record per line; mirrors ThinkAloud's local SQLite schema.
205	        - `audio/<yyyy-mm-dd>/<id>.wav` — 16 kHz mono PCM recordings; path matches each record's `audio_path` field.
206	        """
207	        try Data(text.utf8).write(to: url, options: .atomic)
208	    }
209	}

1	import Foundation
2	import MLX
3	import MLXAudioCore
4	
5	struct BenchmarkResult: Sendable, Identifiable, Codable, Equatable {
6	    let id: String                  // record id
7	    let recordCreatedAt: String
8	    let groundTruth: String         // record.editedTranscript at run time (treated as ground truth)
9	    let predictedRaw: String        // model output before post-process
10	    let predictedEdited: String     // after TranscriptPostProcessor (final pipeline output)
11	
12	    // Strict scoring — whitespace-only normalization. Preserves case + punctuation + full-width.
13	    let editDistance: Int
14	    let referenceLength: Int
15	    let cer: Double
16	    let exactMatch: Bool
17	
18	    // Lenient scoring — aggressive normalization (lowercase, full→half width, strip punctuation).
19	    // Matches the convention Whisper-style ASR benchmarks report. Optional for forward-compat
20	    // with any legacy in-memory report objects that predate this field.
21	    let editDistanceNormalized: Int?
22	    let referenceLengthNormalized: Int?
23	    let cerNormalized: Double?
24	    let exactMatchNormalized: Bool?
25	
26	    let durationMs: Int
27	    let error: String?
28	
29	    var passed: Bool { error == nil }
30	
31	    /// Pick the right metric tuple based on the user's display toggle. Falls back to the strict
32	    /// fields when an older report doesn't carry normalized values.
33	    func cer(useNormalized: Bool) -> Double {
34	        useNormalized ? (cerNormalized ?? cer) : cer
35	    }
36	    func exactMatch(useNormalized: Bool) -> Bool {
37	        useNormalized ? (exactMatchNormalized ?? exactMatch) : exactMatch
38	    }
39	}
40	
41	struct BenchmarkReport: Sendable, Codable {
42	    let modelID: String
43	    let chinesePreference: ChinesePreference
44	    let runAt: String               // ISO8601 timestamp
45	    let results: [BenchmarkResult]
46	
47	    var total: Int { results.count }
48	    var completed: Int { results.filter { $0.error == nil }.count }
49	    var failed: Int { results.filter { $0.error != nil }.count }
50	
51	    func exactMatchCount(useNormalized: Bool) -> Int {
52	        results.filter { $0.exactMatch(useNormalized: useNormalized) }.count
53	    }
54	    func exactMatchRate(useNormalized: Bool) -> Double {
55	        total == 0 ? 0 : Double(exactMatchCount(useNormalized: useNormalized)) / Double(total)
56	    }
57	
58	    func averageCER(useNormalized: Bool) -> Double {
59	        let valid = results.filter { $0.error == nil }
60	        guard !valid.isEmpty else { return 0 }
61	        return valid.map { $0.cer(useNormalized: useNormalized) }.reduce(0, +) / Double(valid.count)
62	    }
63	
64	    var averageLatencyMs: Int {
65	        let valid = results.filter { $0.error == nil }
66	        guard !valid.isEmpty else { return 0 }
67	        return valid.map(\.durationMs).reduce(0, +) / valid.count
68	    }
69	}
70	
71	/// Sendable per-record progress callback payload.
72	struct BenchmarkProgress: Sendable {
73	    let completed: Int
74	    let total: Int
75	    let currentRecordID: String
76	}
77	
78	/// Runs the full transcription pipeline (audio decode → model → post-process) against every
79	/// record in a dataset slice, comparing against the stored editedTranscript. Honors Task
80	/// cancellation between records.
81	actor BenchmarkRunner {
82	    func run(
83	        records: [DatasetRecord],
84	        runtime: any ASRRuntime,
85	        chinesePreference: ChinesePreference,
86	        audioURLProvider: @Sendable (DatasetRecord) async -> URL,
87	        progress: @Sendable (BenchmarkProgress) async -> Void = { _ in }
88	    ) async throws -> BenchmarkReport {
89	        var results: [BenchmarkResult] = []
90	        results.reserveCapacity(records.count)
91	
92	        for (index, record) in records.enumerated() {
93	            try Task.checkCancellation()
94	            await progress(BenchmarkProgress(completed: index, total: records.count, currentRecordID: record.id))
95	
96	            let audioURL = await audioURLProvider(record)
97	            let result = await transcribe(record: record, audioURL: audioURL, runtime: runtime, chinesePreference: chinesePreference)
98	            results.append(result)
99	        }
100	
101	        try Task.checkCancellation()
102	        await progress(BenchmarkProgress(completed: records.count, total: records.count, currentRecordID: ""))
103	
104	        let modelID = await runtime.modelID
105	        let runAt = ISO8601DateFormatter().string(from: Date())
106	        return BenchmarkReport(modelID: modelID, chinesePreference: chinesePreference, runAt: runAt, results: results)
107	    }
108	
109	    private func transcribe(record: DatasetRecord, audioURL: URL, runtime: any ASRRuntime, chinesePreference: ChinesePreference) async -> BenchmarkResult {
110	        let groundTruth = record.editedTranscript
111	        let start = Date()
112	
113	        // Decode audio → [Float] mirroring production pipeline.
114	        let samples: [Float]
115	        do {
116	            let (_, mlxArray) = try loadAudioArray(from: audioURL, sampleRate: 16000)
117	            samples = mlxArray.asArray(Float.self)
118	        } catch {
119	            return failure(record: record, groundTruth: groundTruth, error: error, start: start)
120	        }
121	
122	        // Run the streaming pipeline end-to-end.
123	        var rawText = ""
124	        do {
125	            for try await event in runtime.transcribeStream(samples: samples, sampleRate: 16000, options: ASROptions(language: record.language)) {
126	                try Task.checkCancellation()
127	                switch event {
128	                case .token(let t):
129	                    rawText += t
130	                case .result(let r):
131	                    rawText = r.text  // final result overrides token-by-token concat
132	                }
133	            }
134	        } catch {
135	            return failure(record: record, groundTruth: groundTruth, error: error, start: start)
136	        }
137	
138	        let editedText = TranscriptPostProcessor.apply(chinesePreference, to: rawText)
139	        let elapsedMs = Int(Date().timeIntervalSince(start) * 1000)
140	
141	        let lightRef = TextMetrics.normalize(groundTruth, mode: .light)
142	        let lightHyp = TextMetrics.normalize(editedText, mode: .light)
143	        let lightDist = TextMetrics.editDistance(lightRef, lightHyp)
144	        let lightCER = lightRef.isEmpty ? (lightHyp.isEmpty ? 0 : 1) : Double(lightDist) / Double(lightRef.count)
145	
146	        let aggrRef = TextMetrics.normalize(groundTruth, mode: .aggressive)
147	        let aggrHyp = TextMetrics.normalize(editedText, mode: .aggressive)
148	        let aggrDist = TextMetrics.editDistance(aggrRef, aggrHyp)
149	        let aggrCER = aggrRef.isEmpty ? (aggrHyp.isEmpty ? 0 : 1) : Double(aggrDist) / Double(aggrRef.count)
150	
151	        return BenchmarkResult(
152	            id: record.id,
153	            recordCreatedAt: record.createdAt,
154	            groundTruth: groundTruth,
155	            predictedRaw: rawText,
156	            predictedEdited: editedText,
157	            editDistance: lightDist,
158	            referenceLength: lightRef.count,
159	            cer: lightCER,
160	            exactMatch: lightRef == lightHyp,
161	            editDistanceNormalized: aggrDist,
162	            referenceLengthNormalized: aggrRef.count,
163	            cerNormalized: aggrCER,
164	            exactMatchNormalized: aggrRef == aggrHyp,
165	            durationMs: elapsedMs,
166	            error: nil
167	        )
168	    }
169	
170	    private func failure(record: DatasetRecord, groundTruth: String, error: Error, start: Date) -> BenchmarkResult {
171	        let elapsedMs = Int(Date().timeIntervalSince(start) * 1000)
172	        return BenchmarkResult(
173	            id: record.id,
174	            recordCreatedAt: record.createdAt,
175	            groundTruth: groundTruth,
176	            predictedRaw: "",
177	            predictedEdited: "",
178	            editDistance: 0,
179	            referenceLength: groundTruth.count,
180	            cer: 0,
181	            exactMatch: false,
182	            editDistanceNormalized: 0,
183	            referenceLengthNormalized: groundTruth.count,
184	            cerNormalized: 0,
185	            exactMatchNormalized: false,
186	            durationMs: elapsedMs,
187	            error: String(describing: error)
188	        )
189	    }
190	}

import Foundation
import GRDB

struct DatasetRecord: Codable, FetchableRecord, PersistableRecord, Sendable, Equatable, Identifiable {
    static let databaseTableName = "records"

    var id: String
    var createdAt: String

    var audioPath: String
    var durationMs: Int?
    var sampleRate: Int?
    var channels: Int?

    var sourceAppBundleID: String?
    var sourceAppName: String?

    var asrProvider: String
    var asrModel: String
    var asrRuntime: String
    var asrConfigJSON: String?

    var rawTranscript: String
    var editedTranscript: String

    var inserted: Bool
    var savedToDataset: Bool

    var language: String?
    var metadataJSON: String?

    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
        case id
        case createdAt = "created_at"
        case audioPath = "audio_path"
        case durationMs = "duration_ms"
        case sampleRate = "sample_rate"
        case channels
        case sourceAppBundleID = "source_app_bundle_id"
        case sourceAppName = "source_app_name"
        case asrProvider = "asr_provider"
        case asrModel = "asr_model"
        case asrRuntime = "asr_runtime"
        case asrConfigJSON = "asr_config_json"
        case rawTranscript = "raw_transcript"
        case editedTranscript = "edited_transcript"
        case inserted
        case savedToDataset = "saved_to_dataset"
        case language
        case metadataJSON = "metadata_json"
    }
}

extension DatasetRecord {
    static func generateID(date: Date = Date()) -> String {
        let formatter = DateFormatter()
        formatter.dateFormat = "yyyyMMddHHmmss"
        formatter.timeZone = TimeZone(secondsFromGMT: 0)
        let prefix = formatter.string(from: date)
        let suffix = UUID().uuidString.split(separator: "-").first.map(String.init) ?? "0000"
        return "rec_\(prefix)_\(suffix.lowercased())"
    }
}

1	import Foundation
2	import Observation
3	
4	@MainActor
5	@Observable
6	final class AppContainer {
7	    let permissions: PermissionsService
8	    let hotkeys: HotkeyManager
9	    let modelManager: ModelManager
10	    let datasetStore: DatasetStore
11	    let audioFileStore: AudioFileStore
12	    let recorder: AudioRecorder
13	    let insertion: TextInsertionManager
14	    let coordinator: PopupCoordinator
15	    private(set) var settingsWindow: SettingsWindowController!
16	    private(set) var datasetBrowserWindow: DatasetBrowserWindowController!
17	    let hfTokenStore: HFTokenStore
18	
19	    init() {
20	        let appSupport = AppPaths.applicationSupportDirectory()
21	        let modelsDir = appSupport.appendingPathComponent("models", isDirectory: true)
22	        let audioDir = appSupport.appendingPathComponent("audio", isDirectory: true)
23	        let dbURL = appSupport.appendingPathComponent("dataset.sqlite")
24	
25	        AppPaths.ensureDirectoryExists(appSupport)
26	        AppPaths.ensureDirectoryExists(modelsDir)
27	        AppPaths.ensureDirectoryExists(audioDir)
28	
29	        let permissions = PermissionsService()
30	        let hotkeys = HotkeyManager()
31	        let modelManager = ModelManager(modelsDirectory: modelsDir)
32	        let datasetStore = DatasetStore(databaseURL: dbURL)
33	        let audioFileStore = AudioFileStore(rootDirectory: audioDir)
34	        let recorder = AudioRecorder()
35	        let insertion = TextInsertionManager()
36	        let hfTokenStore = HFTokenStore()
37	
38	        self.permissions = permissions
39	        self.hotkeys = hotkeys
40	        self.modelManager = modelManager
41	        self.datasetStore = datasetStore
42	        self.audioFileStore = audioFileStore
43	        self.recorder = recorder
44	        self.insertion = insertion
45	        self.hfTokenStore = hfTokenStore
46	        self.coordinator = PopupCoordinator(
47	            permissions: permissions,
48	            modelManager: modelManager,
49	            recorder: recorder,
50	            insertion: insertion,
51	            datasetStore: datasetStore,
52	            audioFileStore: audioFileStore
53	        )
54	        self.settingsWindow = SettingsWindowController(container: self)
55	        self.datasetBrowserWindow = DatasetBrowserWindowController(container: self)
56	        self.coordinator.settingsOpener = { [weak self] in
57	            self?.openSettings()
58	        }
59	    }
60	
61	    func openSettings() {
62	        settingsWindow.show()
63	    }
64	
65	    func openDatasetBrowser() {
66	        datasetBrowserWindow.show()
67	    }
68	
69	    func start() {
70	        Task { @MainActor in
71	            do {
72	                try await datasetStore.setup()
73	            } catch {
74	                NSLog("DatasetStore setup failed: \(error)")
75	            }
76	        }
77	        hotkeys.bind(handlers: .init(
78	            startRecording: { [weak coordinator] in coordinator?.invoke() },
79	            stopAndTranscribe: { [weak coordinator] in coordinator?.stopAndTranscribe() },
80	            insertAndSave: { [weak coordinator] in coordinator?.insertAndSave() }
81	        ))
82	    }
83	
84	    func shutdown() {
85	        hotkeys.unbind()
86	    }
87	}

89	enum AppPaths {
90	    static func applicationSupportDirectory() -> URL {
91	        let fm = FileManager.default
92	        let base = (try? fm.url(for: .applicationSupportDirectory, in: .userDomainMask, appropriateFor: nil, create: true))
93	            ?? URL(fileURLWithPath: NSHomeDirectory()).appendingPathComponent("Library/Application Support", isDirectory: true)
94	        return base.appendingPathComponent("ThinkAloud", isDirectory: true)
95	    }
96	
97	    static func ensureDirectoryExists(_ url: URL) {
98	        try? FileManager.default.createDirectory(at: url, withIntermediateDirectories: true)
99	    }
100	}

1	import Foundation
2	import Observation
3	
4	@MainActor
5	@Observable
6	final class HFPushController {
7	    // Form state
8	    var repoName: String = "thinkaloud-personal"
9	    var organization: String = ""
10	    var isPrivate: Bool = true
11	    var includeAudio: Bool = true
12	
13	    // Runtime state
14	    private(set) var isRunning: Bool = false
15	    private(set) var stage: HFPushProgress.Stage = .prepare
16	    private(set) var stageCompleted: Int = 0
17	    private(set) var stageTotal: Int = 0
18	    private(set) var currentLabel: String = ""
19	    private(set) var result: HFPushResult?
20	    private(set) var errorMessage: String?
21	
22	    private let tokenStore: HFTokenStore
23	    private let datasetStore: DatasetStore
24	    private let audioFileStore: AudioFileStore
25	
26	    private var task: Task<Void, Never>?
27	
28	    init(tokenStore: HFTokenStore, datasetStore: DatasetStore, audioFileStore: AudioFileStore) {
29	        self.tokenStore = tokenStore
30	        self.datasetStore = datasetStore
31	        self.audioFileStore = audioFileStore
32	    }
33	
34	    var canPush: Bool {
35	        tokenStore.hasToken && !repoName.isEmpty && !isRunning
36	    }
37	
38	    func push() {
39	        guard !isRunning, let token = tokenStore.token else { return }
40	        let datasetStore = datasetStore
41	        let audioFileStore = audioFileStore
42	        let org = organization.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
43	        let options = HFPushOptions(
44	            repoName: repoName.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines),
45	            organization: org.isEmpty ? nil : org,
46	            isPrivate: isPrivate,
47	            includeAudio: includeAudio
48	        )
49	
50	        isRunning = true
51	        result = nil
52	        errorMessage = nil
53	        stage = .prepare
54	        stageCompleted = 0
55	        stageTotal = 0
56	        currentLabel = ""
57	
58	        task = Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
59	            defer {
60	                self?.isRunning = false
61	                self?.task = nil
62	            }
63	            let client = HFHubClient(token: token)
64	            // Resolve owner from whoami so the user doesn't have to type it.
65	            let owner: String
66	            do {
67	                let me = try await client.whoami()
68	                owner = me.name
69	                self?.tokenStore.verifiedUsername = me.name
70	            } catch {
71	                self?.errorMessage = String(localized: "Auth failed: \(error.localizedDescription)")
72	                return
73	            }
74	            let service = HFPushService(client: client, datasetStore: datasetStore, audioFileStore: audioFileStore, defaultOwner: owner)
75	            do {
76	                let res = try await service.push(options: options) { [weak self] p in
77	                    await MainActor.run {
78	                        self?.stage = p.stage
79	                        self?.stageCompleted = p.completed
80	                        self?.stageTotal = p.total
81	                        self?.currentLabel = p.currentLabel
82	                    }
83	                }
84	                self?.result = res
85	            } catch is CancellationError {
86	                self?.errorMessage = String(localized: "Cancelled.")
87	            } catch {
88	                self?.errorMessage = String(localized: "Push failed: \(error.localizedDescription)")
89	            }
90	        }
91	    }
92	
93	    func cancel() {
94	        task?.cancel()
95	    }
96	}
